executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations publicly breached in the past 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (coinbasecartel), National Money Mart with a confirmed database leak (everest), Peterborough Public Health (lynx), and Eastern Townships School Board (qilin). The Qilin ransomware group alone accounts for 20 Canadian victims, while CLOP has targeted 12 organizations and Play ransomware hit 9. Critical sectors affected include healthcare, education, public sector entities, and financial services—immediate incident response and threat hunting across Canadian networks is advised.

items.name: Qilin (Agenda)
items.targets: 20 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Waterloo Information Systems, Kuzco Lighting, Service Star Freightways, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Pre-Con Builders, CST Coal, Berts Electric, Questica, and multiple law/accounting firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.name: CLOP (Cl0p)
items.targets: 12 Canadian organizations including law firms (SMT Lawyers), technology providers (Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Technikel, ModTech), energy sector (East Plats), hospitality (Granite Pointe, Whiskijack Resorts), manufacturing (Ideal Welders), and helicopter services (CMH Heli)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), typically exploits file transfer vulnerabilities

items.name: Play
items.targets: 9 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Select Tool, Paisley Products, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses, and accounting firm Pewarchuk CPA
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 10 Canadian organizations including Teccart college, agriculture/food (Matt and Steve, Ripobec), construction (Groupe Piche, Kenalex, Silverline Group), manufacturing (Precision Aluminum), transportation (Briway Carriers), and retail (MacTavish Co)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Labtician Ophthalmics (Healthcare), Groupe Caddac (Construction), ControlGMC (Manufacturing), Real Tech (Manufacturing), and Colliers business services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1057 (Process Discovery)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Phoenix Labs, CCR Solutions, Esposito Bros Construction, Soteck, McKee-Pownall Equine Services (Healthcare), and Emond Publishing (Education)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1021 (Remote Services)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health (critical public sector), Trucash (Financial Services), Laurysen Kitchens, Group Echo, and KTL Group (Technology)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System), T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel)

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 7 Canadian organizations including CPK Interior, Excavation Tourigny, Glassers TV, Steelworks Inc, Bison Family Medical (Healthcare), Omega Tool Corp, Bais Yaakov (Education), and accounting firm Reidhurst Nagy
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools)

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company (Canada) - Database leaked (confirmed exfiltration of Financial Services data)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage)

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations: Active Green + Ross (automotive retail), Morison Insurance Brokers (Financial Services), Kids & Company (childcare services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Solutions Extreme Technology and Eeyou Communications Network (Telecommunication - Indigenous network)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Inland Audio Visual (Canadian Business Services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021.001 (Remote Desktop Protocol), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: CoinbaseCartel
items.targets: Canada Goose (major Canadian retail brand in Consumer Services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Regal Building Materials Ltd (Construction) and one undisclosed entity
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods (Canadian Agriculture and Food Production)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Payload
items.targets: Q2 Artificial Lift Services (Canadian Manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: MRC de Maskinongé district (Canadian Public Sector - municipal government)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1489 (Service Stop)

items.name: WorldLeaks
items.targets: Municipality of North Perth (Canadian Public Sector)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Living Realty (Business Services) and Cadman Power Equipment (Manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Axion50plus (Financial Services), Beauséjour Co-op (Agriculture), Ravand (Technology), and one additional redacted entity
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Anubis
items.targets: One undisclosed law firm in Canada (Business Services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery)

items.name: Nitrogen
items.targets: Whitfield Welding Inc (Canadian Manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059.001 (PowerShell)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under severe ransomware assault with 103 organizations publicly breached across every sector in 48 hours. High-impact victims include Canada Goose (retail), National Money Mart (financial - database leaked), two public health entities (Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre), two school boards (Eastern Townships, Bais Yaakov), and two municipalities (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé). No CCCS advisories were issued in this reporting period despite the severity. Critical infrastructure sectors including healthcare, education, telecommunications, and public services are heavily represented.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major apparel brand) breached by CoinbaseCartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) breached by Sinobi ransomware
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loan/financial services) breached by Everest ransomware with confirmed database leak; Morison Insurance Brokers breached by Sinobi; Trucash financial services breached by Lynx; multiple accounting/CPA firms breached (Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, Reidhurst Nagy)

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has issued a 3-day emergency patching directive for a critical Check Point VPN zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-50751) actively exploited by Qilin ransomware operators to bypass authentication on outdated IKEv1 setups. A second critical flaw in LiteLLM (CVE-2026-42271) is also under active exploitation, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued an alert regarding a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories. France's government secure messaging platform Tchap was breached through account hijacking, and multiple data breaches affecting healthcare providers and Australian retailers expose hundreds of thousands of customer records.

items.description: Critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Check Point Remote Access VPN (IKEv1)
items.software_affected: Check Point Remote Access VPN with IKEv1 protocol enabled
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in LiteLLM allowing arbitrary command execution on servers
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: WinRAR vulnerability exploited by Russia-aligned groups to deploy information stealers in Ukraine
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.description: Actively exploited denial of service flaw in SolarWinds
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Low, Integrity: Low, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability exploited by AI worm proof-of-concept to spread across test network
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Medium, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Medium

items.description: Critical remote code execution flaw in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Organizations using Check Point VPN with IKEv1 enabled
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - exploiting CVE-2026-50751 for initial access and authentication bypass

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: Spray Equipment & Service Center, Rockaway River Country Club, Centre Ellipse
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - double extortion tactics

items.name: UNK_DeadDrop (likely North Korean)
items.targets: Developers via GitHub, cryptocurrency holders
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204 (User Execution), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - weaponized GitHub repositories, vishing, physical intrusions, 250+ fake job offers over 6 weeks

items.name: UNC3753 (Iran-linked)
items.targets: U.S. organizations
items.ttps: T1566.004 (Phishing: Spearphishing Voice), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol) - vishing, physical intrusions, Bomgar abuse, data theft extortion campaigns

items.name: NSO Group
items.targets: WhatsApp users globally
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution) - spearphishing attempts linked to Pegasus spyware

items.name: Russia-aligned groups
items.targets: Ukrainian organizations and individuals
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution) - exploiting CVE-2025-8088 WinRAR flaw to deploy information stealers

items.name: Unknown (Tchap breach)
items.targets: French government secure messaging platform Tchap
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1110 (Brute Force) - account hijacking attacks on government communications

items.name: thegentlemen
items.targets: Central Arkansas Pediatrics - medical and health records
items.ttps: T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - threat actor claiming exposure of patient medical records

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-013 on May 29, 2026, warning of a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories, which poses supply chain risks to Canadian developers and organizations using GitHub for code management. No specific incidents targeting major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period, though Canadian organizations using Check Point VPN or LiteLLM are urged to apply emergency patches immediately.
